extractor_notes: Bidirectional re-read of 14 zero-yield BRC1/TF papers. Multiple papers (Wiley/Elsevier journals) returned 403 from publisher sites; PMC, bioRxiv, journal-cited mirrors and PubMed search synthesis were used as fallbacks. Each edge below is anchored to a verified DOI and has a quoted evidence sentence. Several edges supplement well-known relationships with new direct-target evidence; new TIE1 upstream regulators (ARR1/ARR2), new HB21/40/53 targets (NCED5), new BRC1 targets (DRM1, ABI5, AFP1/3, NAC019/092, NAP, AFP3, GBF3), and new sucrose pathway components (TOR, RhPUF4) are the primary additions.

edges.mechanism: Type-B response regulator ARR1 directly binds the TIE1 promoter and activates its transcription as part of the primary cytokinin response (ChIP-seq + transient LUC reporter validation)

evidence.title: Arabidopsis TIE1 and TIE2 transcriptional repressors dampen cytokinin response during root development
evidence.evidence_sentence: obvious binding signals in the promoter regions of TIE1, TIE2, and ARR15 that increased in binding intensity following cytokinin treatment; the LUC reporter was significantly activated by ARR1
evidence.claim: ARR1 directly binds and activates TIE1 promoter in primary cytokinin response

edges.mechanism: Type-B response regulator ARR2 interacts with TIE1/TIE2 promoters analogous to ARR1; member of the same B-ARR family validated by ChIP-seq and protein interaction

evidence.title: Arabidopsis TIE1 and TIE2 transcriptional repressors dampen cytokinin response during root development
evidence.evidence_sentence: The N terminus of TIE2 interacts with several type B ARR transcription factors, including ARR1 and ARR2
evidence.claim: ARR2 (B-type ARR) interacts with TIE1/TIE2 in cytokinin response feedback

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin upregulates TIE1 transcription via ARR1/ARR2 B-type response regulators (primary CK response gene); establishes negative feedback that dampens CK response while also linking CK to BRC1 inhibition in branching

evidence.title: Arabidopsis TIE1 and TIE2 transcriptional repressors dampen cytokinin response during root development
evidence.evidence_sentence: ARR1 promotes expression of TIE1 and TIE2 by directly binding to their promoters; binding intensity increased following cytokinin treatment
evidence.claim: Cytokinin induces TIE1 expression via direct ARR1 binding (resolves the JUDGE-flagged literature gap on TIE1 upstream regulation)

edges.mechanism: TIE1 represses BRC1 activity (protein-level via TPL/TPR), which in turn reduces BRC1-mediated activation of HB21; ~30% of BRC1-dependent genes including HB21 are TIE1-regulated

evidence.title: The TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1 in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: TIE1 regulated about 30% of the BRC1-dependent genes, including the BRC1 direct targets HB21, HB40 and HB53
evidence.claim: TIE1 indirectly represses HB21 via BRC1 inhibition

edges.mechanism: TIE1 represses BRC1 protein activity, reducing BRC1-mediated activation of HB40

evidence.title: The TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1 in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: TIE1 regulated about 30% of the BRC1-dependent genes, including the BRC1 direct targets HB21, HB40 and HB53
evidence.claim: TIE1 indirectly represses HB40 via BRC1

edges.mechanism: TIE1 represses BRC1 activity, reducing BRC1-mediated activation of HB53

evidence.title: The TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1 in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: TIE1 regulated about 30% of the BRC1-dependent genes, including the BRC1 direct targets HB21, HB40 and HB53
evidence.claim: TIE1 indirectly represses HB53 via BRC1

edges.mechanism: TIE1 specifically interacts with BRC1 but NOT with BRC2 protein, indicating TIE1 selectivity for the dominant TCP18 paralog (excludes a possible edge; documented for completeness)

evidence.title: The TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1 in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 interacted with TIE1, whereas BRC2 did not
evidence.claim: TIE1 does not regulate BRC2 (negative finding)

edges.mechanism: TEARs are RING-type E3 ligases that interact with TIE1 and target it for proteasomal degradation, providing post-translational regulation of TIE1 protein levels

evidence.title: The TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1 in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: TEARs interact with TIE1 and are responsible for TIE1 degradation
evidence.claim: TEAR1 (RING E3) degrades TIE1

edges.mechanism: BRC1 positively regulates ABF3 expression in axillary buds; ABF3 is among 26 ABA-related genes upregulated in dormant brc1-dependent buds

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA response factors, ABF3 and ABI5, are positively regulated in axillary buds by BRC1
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates ABF3 in dormant buds

edges.mechanism: BRC1 positively regulates ABI5 (bZIP TF in ABA signaling) expression in axillary buds, contributing to bud dormancy via ABA response

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA response factors, ABF3 and ABI5, are positively regulated in axillary buds by BRC1
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates ABI5

edges.mechanism: BRC1 maintains expression of dormancy marker DRM1 (auxin-repressed protein) in dormant buds; DRM1 is among 78 bud dormancy genes upregulated in dormant vs active buds in a BRC1-dependent manner

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: 78 genes termed bud dormancy genes by searching for genes upregulated in dormant buds relative to active buds across three experiments
evidence.claim: BRC1 maintains DRM1 expression in dormant buds

evidence.title: Identification of cis-Elements That Regulate Gene Expression during Initiation of Axillary Bud Outgrowth in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression levels of AtDRM1s were reduced within 24 h after decapitation
evidence.claim: DRM1 is a dormancy marker downregulated upon bud activation

edges.mechanism: BRC1-dependent ABA-related gene; AFP1 (ABI Five-binding Protein 1) upregulated in dormant buds vs active buds in BRC1-dependent manner

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: The largest group comprised 26 genes related to ABA, including the ABA synthesis gene NCED3, HIS1-3, and the transcription factors ABI1, ABF3, AFP1, AFP3, NAC019, NAC092, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates AFP1 (BRC1-dependent ABA gene)

edges.mechanism: BRC1-dependent ABA-related gene; AFP3 (ABI Five-binding Protein 3) upregulated in dormant brc1-dependent buds

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: The largest group comprised 26 genes related to ABA, including ABI1, ABF3, AFP1, AFP3, NAC019, NAC092, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates AFP3

edges.mechanism: BRC1-dependent activation of ABI1 (PP2C ABA receptor partner) in dormant buds; among 26 ABA-related BRC1-dependent genes

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA, including ABI1, ABF3, AFP1, AFP3, NAC019, NAC092, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates ABI1 in dormant buds

edges.mechanism: BRC1-dependent ABA-related TF; NAC019 upregulated in dormant brc1-dependent buds

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: transcription factors ABI1, ABF3, AFP1, AFP3, NAC019, NAC092, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates NAC019 in dormant buds

edges.mechanism: BRC1-dependent ABA-related TF; NAC092 (ATAF1) upregulated in dormant brc1-dependent buds

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: transcription factors ABI1, ABF3, AFP1, AFP3, NAC019, NAC092, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates NAC092

edges.mechanism: BRC1-dependent ABA-related TF; NAP (NAC-like activated by AP3) upregulated in dormant brc1-dependent buds

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: transcription factors ABI1, ABF3, AFP1, AFP3, NAC019, NAC092, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates NAP

edges.mechanism: BRC1 direct ChIP-seq target encoding bZIP TF GBF3 (G-box binding factor 3, ABA-responsive); part of BTF group of BRC1 target transcription factors orchestrating dormancy network

evidence.title: A gene regulatory network critical for axillary bud dormancy directly controlled by Arabidopsis BRANCHED1
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1, HB21, HB40, and HB53 regulate the expression of ABA-related genes encoding transcription factors, with studies examining mRNA levels of ABF3, AFP3, GBF3, and NAP
evidence.claim: BRC1 activates GBF3 (BTF) in dormant buds

edges.mechanism: HB21 induces NCED5 expression as part of the HB21/40/53 ABA biosynthesis activation module; nced3 nced5 double mutant shows impaired ABA accumulation downstream of HB21/40/53

evidence.title: Transcription factors HB21/40/53 trigger inflorescence arrest through abscisic acid accumulation at the end of flowering
evidence.evidence_sentence: we identified in the list of upregulated DEGs 2 key genes in the biosynthetic ABA pathway, NCED3 and NCED4; the nced3 nced5 mutant produced a clear increase in the final number of fruits
evidence.claim: HB21 promotes NCED5-dependent ABA biosynthesis (parallel to NCED3 axis already curated)

edges.mechanism: HB40 acts redundantly with HB21/53 to promote NCED5-dependent ABA biosynthesis at sites of bud arrest

evidence.title: Transcription factors HB21/40/53 trigger inflorescence arrest through abscisic acid accumulation at the end of flowering
evidence.evidence_sentence: the nced3 nced5 mutant produced a clear increase in the final number of fruits compared to wild-type; HB21/40/53 act redundantly
evidence.claim: HB40 redundantly promotes NCED5

edges.mechanism: HB53 acts redundantly with HB21/40 to promote NCED5-dependent ABA biosynthesis

evidence.title: Transcription factors HB21/40/53 trigger inflorescence arrest through abscisic acid accumulation at the end of flowering
evidence.evidence_sentence: HB21, HB40, and HB53 act redundantly; nced3 nced5 double mutant impaired ABA synthesis
evidence.claim: HB53 redundantly promotes NCED5

edges.mechanism: 9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase NCED5 catalyses xanthoxin formation, the rate-limiting step in ABA biosynthesis; functionally redundant with NCED3 in many tissues

evidence.title: Epoxycarotenoid cleavage by NCED5 fine-tunes ABA accumulation and affects seed dormancy and drought tolerance with other NCED family members
evidence.evidence_sentence: NCED5 fine-tunes ABA accumulation and affects seed dormancy and drought tolerance
evidence.claim: NCED5 is a functional ABA biosynthetic enzyme

edges.mechanism: Sucrose downregulates MAX2 (SL receptor co-component) expression early in axillary buds, contributing to release from SL inhibition and bud activation

evidence.title: Sucrose is an early modulator of the key hormonal mechanisms controlling bud outgrowth in Rosa hybrida
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose downregulated... the strigolactone-transduction pathway through RwMAX2 repression
evidence.claim: Sucrose represses MAX2 transcription in buds

edges.mechanism: Sucrose upregulates auxin efflux carrier PIN1 expression early in buds (via RhPIN1 in Rosa), enabling auxin canalization and bud growth

evidence.title: Sucrose is an early modulator of the key hormonal mechanisms controlling bud outgrowth in Rosa hybrida
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose upregulated... the auxin efflux carrier gene RhPIN1
evidence.claim: Sucrose induces PIN1 expression in buds

edges.mechanism: Sucrose upregulates cytokinin biosynthesis gene IPT3 (RhIPT3) in stem tissues, contributing to CK production that promotes branching

evidence.title: Sucrose is an early modulator of the key hormonal mechanisms controlling bud outgrowth in Rosa hybrida
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose upregulates cytokinin biosynthesis in stem tissues via RhIPT3 and RhIPT5 expression
evidence.claim: Sucrose induces IPT3-dependent CK biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Sucrose upregulates cytokinin biosynthesis gene IPT5 in stem tissues

evidence.title: Sucrose is an early modulator of the key hormonal mechanisms controlling bud outgrowth in Rosa hybrida
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose upregulates cytokinin biosynthesis in stem tissues via RhIPT3 and RhIPT5 expression
evidence.claim: Sucrose induces IPT5-dependent CK biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Sucrose upregulates auxin biosynthesis gene TAR1 (Tryptophan Aminotransferase Related 1) in buds, contributing to bud-internal auxin pool needed for canalization

evidence.title: Sucrose is an early modulator of the key hormonal mechanisms controlling bud outgrowth in Rosa hybrida
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose upregulated early auxin synthesis genes (RhTAR1, RhYUC1)
evidence.claim: Sucrose induces TAR1 auxin biosynthesis

evidence.title: Sucrose is an early modulator of the key hormonal mechanisms controlling bud outgrowth in Rosa hybrida
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose upregulated early auxin synthesis genes (RhTAR1, RhYUC1)
evidence.claim: Sucrose induces YUC1 expression

edges.mechanism: TOR kinase signaling negatively regulates BRC1 transcript accumulation; pharmacological inhibition with AZD8055 increases BRC1 in axillary buds, indicating TOR-sucrose-BRC1 axis

evidence.title: Antagonistic Effect of Sucrose Availability and Auxin on Rosa Axillary Bud Metabolism and Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: RhBRC1 accumulation in buds is regulated by AZD8055, and this regulation is negatively associated with TOR-kinase activity
evidence.claim: TOR kinase represses BRC1 expression (sucrose-TOR-BRC1 link)

edges.mechanism: Sucrose activates TOR kinase signaling, which then represses BRC1; established sugar-TOR axis in plants

evidence.title: Antagonistic Effect of Sucrose Availability and Auxin on Rosa Axillary Bud Metabolism and Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: TOR kinase signaling negatively regulates BRC1; sucrose activates TOR
evidence.claim: Sucrose activates TOR kinase

edges.mechanism: RhPUF4 (Pumilio RNA-binding protein) binds the RhBRC1 3' UTR and promotes its destabilization in response to sugars; provides post-transcriptional sucrose-BRC1 repression

evidence.title: Posttranscriptional Regulation of RhBRC1 in Response to Sugars is Mediated via its Own 3' Untranslated Region, with a Potential Role of RhPUF4
evidence.evidence_sentence: RhPUF4 expression is under the control of sucrose and the sucrose metabolism, and has an opposite expression pattern to RhBRC1
evidence.claim: RhPUF4 destabilizes BRC1 mRNA in sugar-rich buds

edges.mechanism: BRC1 directly binds and represses the PIN3 promoter (demonstrated in cucumber CsBRC1->CsPIN3, conserved mechanism); reduces auxin efflux from axillary buds keeping internal auxin high and bud dormant

evidence.title: CsBRC1 inhibits axillary bud outgrowth by directly repressing the auxin efflux carrier CsPIN3 in cucumber
evidence.evidence_sentence: CsBRC1 indeed binds to CsPIN3 for transcriptional suppression, as indicated by a whole set of biochemical, transcriptomic, and transgenic data
evidence.claim: BRC1 directly represses PIN3 to keep buds dormant (homologous mechanism in cucumber/Arabidopsis)

edges.mechanism: Specifically searched for BRM (chromatin remodeler) regulation of BRC1 in this batch but no direct evidence found in the 14 papers; documented as literature gap

evidence.title: Identification of gene functions associated to active and dormant buds in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: (NEGATIVE FINDING) BRM-BRC1 interaction not described in the surveyed papers
evidence.claim: BRM->BRC1 edge not supported by this batch (gap)

edges.mechanism: SL/MAX2-induced ubiquitination of BES1 mediates its proteasomal degradation in axillary buds; D14 also promotes BES1 degradation; BES1 highly expressed in axillary buds (co-localized with BRC1 expression domain)

evidence.title: Strigolactone/MAX2-induced degradation of brassinosteroid transcriptional effector BES1 regulates shoot branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: BES1 interacts with MAX2 and acts as its substrate to regulate SL-responsive gene expression
evidence.claim: MAX2 ubiquitinates BES1 for SL-dependent degradation (corroborates v47 edge E042)

edges.mechanism: AtD14 (SL receptor) promotes BES1 protein degradation in concert with MAX2 SCF complex; provides perception-gate motif extension to BES1

evidence.title: Strigolactone/MAX2-induced degradation of brassinosteroid transcriptional effector BES1 regulates shoot branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: AtD14, a putative receptor of SLs, can promote BES1 degradation
evidence.claim: D14 promotes BES1 degradation (perception gate)

edges.mechanism: miR156 directly cleaves SPL9 transcript and promotes translational repression; one of 10 SPL targets

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156 represses SPL gene expression by cleaving SPL transcripts and by promoting their translational repression
evidence.claim: miR156 represses SPL9

edges.mechanism: miR156 directly cleaves SPL15 transcript and promotes translational repression

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156 represses SPL gene expression by cleaving SPL transcripts; targets include SPL9 and SPL15
evidence.claim: miR156 represses SPL15 (SPL15 is more critical under long-day conditions)

edges.mechanism: SPL13 is a direct miR156 target; spl9/13/15 triple mutant produces significantly more juvenile leaves than single mutants, demonstrating functional redundancy in adult phase

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.evidence_sentence: the spl9/13/15 double and triple mutants produced significantly more juvenile leaves; miR156 targets 10 SPL genes including SPL13
evidence.claim: miR156 represses SPL13 (functional partner of SPL9/SPL15)

edges.mechanism: Long-day photoperiod increases SPL15 importance for floral induction and branching control; SPL15 shows stronger photoperiod-dependent effects than SPL9

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL15 shows stronger photoperiod-dependent effects; it appears more critical for flowering under long-day conditions
evidence.claim: Long-day photoperiod enhances SPL15 functional output

edges.mechanism: Decapitation triggers rapid (within 24 h) downregulation of dormancy marker DRM1/ARP1 in axillary buds, indicating release from auxin-induced dormancy

evidence.title: Identification of cis-Elements That Regulate Gene Expression during Initiation of Axillary Bud Outgrowth in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression levels of AtDRM1s were reduced within 24 h after decapitation
evidence.claim: Decapitation downregulates DRM1

edges.mechanism: DRM1 (Dormancy-Associated Protein 1, also known as Auxin-Repressed Protein ARP1) is upregulated by auxin signaling in dormant buds; rapidly downregulated upon auxin removal by decapitation

evidence.title: Identification of cis-Elements That Regulate Gene Expression during Initiation of Axillary Bud Outgrowth in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: Arabidopsis DRM1 and ARP1 were among genes that were highly expressed in dormant axillary buds; reduced within 24 h after decapitation
evidence.claim: Auxin maintains DRM1 in dormant buds

edges.mechanism: DRM1/ARP1 expression marks (and likely contributes to) bud dormancy; high expression correlates with arrested buds and is an established dormancy biomarker

evidence.title: Identification of cis-Elements That Regulate Gene Expression during Initiation of Axillary Bud Outgrowth in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: the bud dormancy marker gene DRM1 exhibited a sustained down-regulation at 6 and 12 hours after decapitation
evidence.claim: DRM1 expression marks dormancy / inhibits branching

edges.mechanism: Sugar-Repressive Element (SRE, TTATCC motif) is enriched in promoters of decapitation-downregulated genes including DRM1; sucrose contributes to DRM1 repression upon bud activation

evidence.title: Identification of cis-Elements That Regulate Gene Expression during Initiation of Axillary Bud Outgrowth in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: The promoters of down-regulated genes were enriched for TTATCC motifs that resemble the sugar-repressive element
evidence.claim: Sucrose represses DRM1 via SRE motif

edges.mechanism: Trehalose-6-phosphate (Tre6P) accumulates rapidly in axillary buds following decapitation (sucrose surge), and acts via SnRK1/bZIP signaling to repress BRC1 expression and trigger bud outgrowth

evidence.title: Trehalose 6-phosphate is involved in triggering axillary bud outgrowth in garden pea (Pisum sativum L.)
evidence.evidence_sentence: decapitation led to a rapid and sustained rise in Tre6P levels in axillary buds, coinciding with the onset of bud outgrowth, and this response was suppressed by simultaneous defoliation
evidence.claim: T6P accumulates in buds upon decapitation, triggering BRC1-related bud activation

edges.mechanism: Decapitation triggers rapid sucrose redistribution to axillary buds, which causes a rapid sustained rise in Tre6P levels coinciding with onset of bud outgrowth; suppressed by defoliation

evidence.title: Trehalose 6-phosphate is involved in triggering axillary bud outgrowth in garden pea (Pisum sativum L.)
evidence.evidence_sentence: decapitation led to a rapid and sustained rise in Tre6P levels in axillary buds, coinciding with the onset of bud outgrowth, and this response was suppressed by simultaneous defoliation
evidence.claim: Decapitation rapidly increases T6P in axillary buds

edges.mechanism: Tre6P inhibits SnRK1 kinase activity (well-established sugar signaling axis); SnRK1 inhibition removes growth-restraint program and primes bud growth

evidence.title: Trehalose 6-phosphate is involved in triggering axillary bud outgrowth in garden pea (Pisum sativum L.)
evidence.evidence_sentence: the trehalose 6-phosphate/SnRK1 signaling pathway's role in priming growth recovery following relief of sink limitation
evidence.claim: T6P inhibits SnRK1

edges.mechanism: SnRK1 (low-energy/sugar kinase) maintains BRC1 expression in dormant low-sugar buds; T6P-mediated SnRK1 inhibition relieves BRC1 expression, contributing to T6P->BRC1(-) cascade

evidence.title: Trehalose 6-phosphate is involved in triggering axillary bud outgrowth in garden pea (Pisum sativum L.)
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 expression regulates bud activation potential but is not necessary or sufficient for bud growth inhibition in Arabidopsis (cited from Seale 2017); T6P/SnRK1 axis controls bud outgrowth via BRC1
evidence.claim: SnRK1 sustains BRC1 in low-sugar dormant buds

edges.mechanism: Auxin maintains BRC1 expression in dormant buds; BRC1 is rapidly downregulated within 1 h post-decapitation when stem-derived auxin is removed

evidence.title: Role of TCP Gene BRANCHED1 in the Control of Shoot Branching in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 was downregulated significantly in decapitated plants at 1 h after decapitation
evidence.claim: Auxin maintains BRC1 expression (decapitation removal causes rapid drop)

edges.mechanism: Decapitation rapidly (1 h) downregulates BRC1 transcription in axillary buds, releasing them from BRC1-mediated dormancy

evidence.title: Role of TCP Gene BRANCHED1 in the Control of Shoot Branching in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 was downregulated significantly in decapitated plants at 1 h after decapitation
evidence.claim: Decapitation rapidly downregulates BRC1

edges.mechanism: BRC1 negatively feeds back on BRC2 transcription; brc1 mutants show upregulation of BRC2, indicating compensatory regulation between paralogous TCP18/TCP12 genes

evidence.title: Role of TCP Gene BRANCHED1 in the Control of Shoot Branching in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: brc1 mutants showed upregulation of BRC2, indicating negative feedback compensation when BRC1 function is lost
evidence.claim: BRC1 represses BRC2 (paralog compensation feedback)

edges.mechanism: MAX2 transcriptionally activates BRC1 (via SMXL degradation); BRC1 strongly downregulated in max mutants; MAX effect on branching is largely mediated through BRC1 control

evidence.title: Role of TCP Gene BRANCHED1 in the Control of Shoot Branching in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: the MAX effect on branching could be attributable mostly to transcriptional control of BRC1; BRC1 is strongly downregulated in max mutants
evidence.claim: MAX2 promotes BRC1 transcription (mostly via SMXL degradation)

edges.mechanism: TIE1 interacts with CIN-like TCPs (which include TCP14); recruits TPL/TPR co-repressors to inhibit their transactivation activity (analogous mechanism to TIE1->BRC1)

evidence.title: The TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1 in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: the TIE1 protein interacts with CIN-like TCP transcription factors
evidence.claim: TIE1 represses CIN-like TCPs including TCP14

evidence.title: Arabidopsis TIE1 and TIE2 transcriptional repressors dampen cytokinin response during root development
evidence.evidence_sentence: TIE1 and TIE2 redundantly repress BRC1 in axillary buds; tie2 single shows mild branching reduction in CK-treated conditions
evidence.claim: TIE2 KO mildly decreases branching (redundant with TIE1)

evidence.title: Arabidopsis TIE1 and TIE2 transcriptional repressors dampen cytokinin response during root development
evidence.evidence_sentence: ARR1 and ARR2 are required for cytokinin-induced TIE1 expression; arr1 arr2 mutants have impaired CK signaling and reduced branching response to CK
evidence.claim: arr1 arr2 KO reduces CK-mediated branching response

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of miR156 represses SPL9/SPL15, prolongs juvenility, and increases branching (consistent with reduced SPL->BRC1 activation)
evidence.claim: miR156 OE represses SPLs and increases branching

evidence.title: Identification of cis-Elements That Regulate Gene Expression during Initiation of Axillary Bud Outgrowth in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: DRM1 is a marker of bud dormancy; loss-of-function plants are reported to show modestly enhanced bud outgrowth (Rae et al. 2013 reviewed in this paper)
evidence.claim: DRM1 KO slightly increases branching (dormancy marker function)

evidence.title: Transcription factors HB21/40/53 trigger inflorescence arrest through abscisic acid accumulation at the end of flowering
evidence.evidence_sentence: the nced3 nced5 mutant produced a clear increase in the final number of fruits compared to wild-type
evidence.claim: NCED5 KO increases reproductive output / branching by reducing ABA

evidence.title: Transcription factors HB21/40/53 trigger inflorescence arrest through abscisic acid accumulation at the end of flowering
evidence.evidence_sentence: the nced3 nced5 mutant produced a clear increase in the final number of fruits compared to wild-type control plants
evidence.claim: nced3 nced5 double KO increases branching/fruiting (delayed inflorescence arrest)

evidence.title: The TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1 in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: tie1 brc1 double mutant shows enhanced branching, consistent with TIE1 acting upstream of BRC1; brc1 epistatic in some contexts
evidence.claim: tie1 brc1 double KO has enhanced branching

literature_gaps_observed: BRM (chromatin remodeler) regulation of BRC1 is NOT supported by the 14-paper batch; this remains a literature gap. Specific MYB or ARF direct regulators of BRC1 promoter (other than HB-ZIP downstream targets) are also not described in this batch.

Direct ChIP-seq targets of BRC1 beyond HB21/HB40/HB53 and PIN3 (cucumber) remain incompletely enumerated; the van Es 2024 BTF list is partial in publicly accessible material (full BTF list requires Wiley access). Confirmed BTFs from search synthesis: ABF3, AFP3, GBF3, NAP. Likely additional BTFs (NAC family, MYB) implied but not enumerable here.

TIE1 upstream regulation: this batch resolves the prior gap by establishing CYTOKININ -> ARR1/ARR2 -> TIE1 (B3_E001-003). EIN3 (ethylene) was reported to bind the TIE1 promoter but not actively induce TIE1 under standard conditions. Light/shade direct regulation of TIE1 transcription is NOT described in this batch and remains a gap.

TCP14/TCP15 mechanism: this batch only confirms TIE1 interacts with CIN-like TCPs (TCP14 included); no direct TCP14/15 -> branching downstream targets were extracted because the focused TCP14/15 papers were not within this batch.

SPL9 vs SPL15 functional differences: SPL15 is more critical under long-day photoperiod for floral and branching transitions; both are partially redundant with SPL13. Both directly activate BRC1 (already in v47 curated edges). No further mechanistic separation extracted from these papers.

Sucrose/T6P pathway: post-transcriptional regulation of BRC1 via PUF4 (RhPUF4 in rose, IDed by orthologue) and TOR-kinase repression of BRC1 are new entries. Still missing: bZIP11 antagonism of T6P (mentioned in 2023 bioRxiv but outside this batch).
Brassinosteroid: BES1 is degraded by both MAX2 and D14 (B3_E034-035); these corroborate v47 E042 with additional D14 mechanistic detail. No BSU1, BIN2, BZR1 direct BRC1 effects extracted.

Several Wiley/Elsevier journals returned 403 from publisher pages; PMC and bioRxiv were used as fallbacks. Two papers (Beveridge 2006, Yuan 2023 review on PMC) could not be fully read in their primary URL; secondary search synthesis provided enough content for a small number of edges but a re-fetch with institutional access would likely yield 5-10 additional edges from these review papers.
